title: L001: Add Chaos Mesh FluxCD deployment infra
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for '# GitHub Issue #132: Add negative and chaos E2E te'

Install Chaos Mesh into the kind cluster via FluxCD, following existing HelmRepository/HelmRelease conventions.

1. **`deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml`** — HelmRepository CR pointing to Chaos Mesh chart registry (`interval: 1h`, matching `openbao.yaml`/`cert-manager.yaml` pattern)
2. **`deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`** — HelmRelease CR with `interval: 30m`, `dependsOn: [cert-manager]`, `install.crds: CreateReplace`, `upgrade.crds: CreateReplace`, `upgrade.remediation.retries: 3`, targeting `chaos-mesh` namespace (following `fluxcd-helmrelease-common-installupgrade-settings` pattern)
3. **`deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml`** — Add `chaos-mesh` namespace declaration (following `two-phase-kustomization-for-crd-dependent-resources` pattern)
4. **`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml`** — Add chaos-mesh source and release to resource list
5. **`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml`** — Kind-specific overlay patch for Chaos Mesh (minimal resource limits, DNS config for kind networking)
6. **`hack/deploy-infra.sh`** — Add `chaos-mesh` to Phase 2 `wait_for_helmreleases` list alongside existing operators (using existing `wait_for_helmreleases` helper, matching `infrastructure-deployment-script-with-8-step-orchestration` pattern)

All files use SPDX Apache-2.0 headers per `spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files` pattern.

**Rationale:** Chaos Mesh is the prerequisite for all chaos test scenarios. Without it installed and healthy in the kind cluster, no PodChaos or NetworkChaos CRs can be applied. This must land first so subsequent features can reference Chaos Mesh CRDs.

**Affected Areas:**
- deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml
- deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml
- deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml
- deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml
- deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml
- hack/deploy-infra.sh

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys Chaos Mesh via FluxCD
stories.want: Chaos Mesh installed in the kind cluster via FluxCD HelmRepository and HelmRelease following the same conventions as cert-manager, openbao, and other operators
stories.so_that: I can apply PodChaos and NetworkChaos CRs in subsequent chaos test scenarios without manual Helm installation
criteria: HelmRepository CR at deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml with interval 1h pointing to https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
HelmRelease CR at deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml with interval 30m, dependsOn cert-manager, install.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3
chaos-mesh namespace declared in deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml
Both source and release listed in deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml resources

stories.title: CI pipeline waits for Chaos Mesh readiness before proceeding
stories.want: the deploy-infra.sh script to include chaos-mesh in its Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases list
stories.so_that: subsequent test steps only run after Chaos Mesh is fully operational and its CRDs are registered
criteria: chaos-mesh added to the Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases call in hack/deploy-infra.sh alongside prometheus-operator-crds, openbao, mariadb-operator-crds, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator
No new timeout variables needed — existing HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT applies
deploy-infra.sh remains idempotent when re-run with chaos-mesh already deployed

stories.title: Kind cluster runs Chaos Mesh with minimal resources
stories.want: a kind-specific overlay patch for Chaos Mesh that reduces resource requests and configures containerd runtime settings
stories.so_that: Chaos Mesh runs in the resource-constrained kind cluster without consuming excessive CPU/memory and can inject faults into containerd-managed containers
criteria: Kustomize patch in deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml targets the chaos-mesh HelmRelease
Patch sets chaosDaemon.runtime to containerd and socketPath to /run/containerd/containerd.sock
Patch disables the dashboard (not needed in CI)
Patch reduces controller-manager and chaos-daemon resource requests to kind-appropriate levels

stories.title: E2E infra health test validates Chaos Mesh deployment
stories.want: the infra-stack-health Chainsaw test to assert that Chaos Mesh controller is running with available replicas
stories.so_that: any deployment regression in Chaos Mesh is caught by the existing e2e-infra CI job before chaos tests run
criteria: New assert step in tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml checking chaos-mesh controller-manager Deployment has availableReplicas > 0
Assert targets namespace chaos-mesh and Deployment name chaos-controller-manager
Test follows existing assertion pattern with JMESPath condition syntax

stories.title: Infrastructure reference documentation covers Chaos Mesh
stories.want: the infrastructure-manifests.md reference doc updated with Chaos Mesh HelmRepository, HelmRelease, and namespace entries
stories.so_that: new team members can find the registry URL, chart version constraint, dependency order, and namespace purpose in one place
criteria: docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md directory layout updated to include sources/chaos-mesh.yaml and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml
Namespaces table updated with chaos-mesh namespace and its purpose
HelmRepository sources table updated with chaos-mesh entry
HelmRelease section updated with Chaos Mesh operator details including dependency order diagram

stories.title: All new files carry SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers
stories.want: all new and modified YAML files to carry the standard 3-line SPDX Apache-2.0 header
stories.so_that: the project maintains license compliance across all infrastructure manifests
criteria: sources/chaos-mesh.yaml starts with SPDX header (lines 1-3: copyright, blank comment, license)
releases/chaos-mesh.yaml starts with SPDX header
Modified files (namespaces.yaml, kustomization.yaml, etc.) retain their existing SPDX headers unchanged

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a HelmRepository CR for Chaos Mesh pointing to the official chart registry at https://charts.chaos-mesh.org with interval 1h, in the flux-system namespace, following the pattern established by sources/cert-manager.yaml and sources/openbao.yaml
requirements.rationale: FluxCD requires a HelmRepository source to pull chart artifacts. The Chaos Mesh official registry is the canonical source. Interval 1h matches all existing HelmRepository sources.

scenarios.name: HelmRepository created with correct spec
scenarios.then: a HelmRepository named chaos-mesh exists in flux-system namespace with url https://charts.chaos-mesh.org and interval 1h

scenarios.name: kustomize build includes the source
scenarios.then: the rendered output contains a HelmRepository resource named chaos-mesh
and_then: no YAML syntax errors are reported

scenarios.name: SPDX header present
scenarios.then: lines 1-3 contain the SPDX Apache-2.0 copyright and license header

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a HelmRelease CR for Chaos Mesh with interval 30m, dependsOn cert-manager, install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3, targeting the chaos-mesh namespace
requirements.rationale: The HelmRelease must follow the fluxcd-helmrelease-common-installupgrade-settings pattern established by all existing HelmReleases. Chaos Mesh depends on cert-manager for webhook TLS certificates.

scenarios.name: HelmRelease follows standard pattern
scenarios.then: a HelmRelease named chaos-mesh exists in chaos-mesh namespace with interval 30m, dependsOn cert-manager/cert-manager, install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3

scenarios.name: Chart source reference is correct
scenarios.then: chart.spec.sourceRef references HelmRepository chaos-mesh in namespace flux-system
and_then: chart name is chaos-mesh with a semver version constraint

scenarios.name: HelmRelease waits for cert-manager
scenarios.when: cert-manager HelmRelease is not yet Ready
scenarios.then: chaos-mesh HelmRelease remains in a pending state and does not attempt installation
and_then: once cert-manager becomes Ready, chaos-mesh installation proceeds

requirements.description: The system SHALL declare a chaos-mesh Namespace resource in deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml following the two-phase-kustomization-for-crd-dependent-resources pattern
requirements.rationale: Explicit Namespace resources must exist before kubectl apply -k creates namespaced HelmRelease CRs. The Namespace is applied first by kustomize resource ordering.

scenarios.name: Namespace resource added
scenarios.then: a Namespace resource with metadata.name chaos-mesh is present as a YAML document
and_then: the format matches existing Namespace declarations (apiVersion v1, kind Namespace)

scenarios.name: Namespace exists before HelmRelease
scenarios.then: the Namespace chaos-mesh appears in the output before the HelmRelease chaos-mesh

scenarios.name: No duplicate namespace
scenarios.then: chaos-mesh namespace appears exactly once

requirements.description: The system SHALL list sources/chaos-mesh.yaml and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml in deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml resources
requirements.rationale: The kustomization.yaml is the entry point for all base FluxCD manifests. Without these entries, the Chaos Mesh resources would not be included in kustomize build output.

scenarios.name: Source and release in kustomization
scenarios.then: sources/chaos-mesh.yaml appears in the HelmRepository sources section and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml appears in the HelmRelease operators section
and_then: ordering follows alphabetical convention within each section

scenarios.name: kustomize build succeeds
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0 and the output contains both the HelmRepository and HelmRelease for chaos-mesh

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a kind-specific kustomize patch for the Chaos Mesh HelmRelease that configures containerd runtime, disables the dashboard, and sets minimal resource requests suitable for a single-node kind cluster
requirements.rationale: Kind clusters use containerd as the container runtime. Chaos Mesh's chaos-daemon must be configured to interact with containerd's socket. Resource limits must be reduced for the constrained kind environment. The dashboard is unnecessary in CI.

scenarios.name: Kind overlay patches chaos-mesh HelmRelease
scenarios.then: the Chaos Mesh HelmRelease values include chaosDaemon.runtime=containerd, chaosDaemon.socketPath=/run/containerd/containerd.sock, and dashboard.create=false
and_then: resource requests are reduced to kind-appropriate levels

scenarios.name: Patch target is correct
scenarios.then: the patch target matches group helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io, version v2, kind HelmRelease, name chaos-mesh, namespace chaos-mesh
and_then: patch format follows the strategic merge patch pattern used by existing patches

scenarios.name: kustomize build with kind overlay succeeds
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0 and the Chaos Mesh HelmRelease includes the patched values

requirements.description: The deployment script (hack/deploy-infra.sh) SHALL include chaos-mesh in the Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases call so that deployment only proceeds after Chaos Mesh is Ready
requirements.rationale: Without waiting for Chaos Mesh readiness, subsequent chaos test scenarios may attempt to apply PodChaos/NetworkChaos CRs before the Chaos Mesh CRDs are registered, causing failures.

scenarios.name: chaos-mesh in wait list
scenarios.then: the wait_for_helmreleases call includes chaos-mesh in the release name list
and_then: script does not proceed to Step 5 until chaos-mesh HelmRelease shows Ready=True

scenarios.name: Timeout applies to chaos-mesh
scenarios.when: chaos-mesh HelmRelease does not become Ready within HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT seconds
scenarios.then: the script exits with error code 1 and logs a timeout message

scenarios.name: Script remains idempotent
scenarios.when: hack/deploy-infra.sh is run a second time with chaos-mesh already deployed and Ready
scenarios.then: the wait_for_helmreleases check passes immediately for chaos-mesh
and_then: no errors or warnings about chaos-mesh already existing

requirements.description: All new YAML files SHALL carry the SPDX Apache-2.0 license header as the first 3 lines, per the spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files pattern
requirements.rationale: License compliance requires all infrastructure files to carry the SPDX header. The pattern is established across all existing files in deploy/.

scenarios.name: New source file has SPDX header
scenarios.then: line 1 is '# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company', line 2 is '#', line 3 is '# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0'

scenarios.name: New release file has SPDX header
scenarios.then: the same 3-line SPDX header is present on lines 1-3

requirements.description: The infra-stack-health E2E test SHALL assert that the Chaos Mesh controller-manager Deployment has available replicas in the chaos-mesh namespace
requirements.rationale: The existing infra-stack-health Chainsaw test validates all deployed operator Deployments. Adding Chaos Mesh ensures deployment regressions are caught by the e2e-infra CI job.

scenarios.name: Chaos Mesh controller asserted in E2E
scenarios.when: the infra-stack-health Chainsaw test runs after deploy-infra.sh completes
scenarios.then: the test includes an assert for Deployment chaos-controller-manager in namespace chaos-mesh with (availableReplicas > 0) == true
and_then: the assertion follows the same JMESPath pattern as existing Deployment checks

scenarios.name: Test fails if Chaos Mesh is not deployed
scenarios.when: the Chaos Mesh HelmRelease is removed or fails to install
scenarios.then: the infra-stack-health test fails with a clear assertion error indicating chaos-controller-manager Deployment is missing or has 0 available replicas

requirements.description: The infrastructure reference documentation (docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md) SHALL be updated with Chaos Mesh HelmRepository, HelmRelease, and namespace entries
requirements.rationale: The reference doc is the single source of truth for infrastructure manifest documentation. Omitting Chaos Mesh would leave a gap for developers looking up registry URLs, version constraints, and dependency order.

scenarios.name: Documentation includes Chaos Mesh source
scenarios.then: the HelmRepository sources table includes a row for chaos-mesh with registry URL and type
and_then: the directory layout diagram includes sources/chaos-mesh.yaml and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml

scenarios.name: Documentation includes Chaos Mesh release
scenarios.then: a Chaos Mesh subsection exists with target namespace, chart, version constraint, source, dependencies, and Helm values
and_then: the dependency order diagram shows chaos-mesh depending on cert-manager

scenarios.name: Documentation includes chaos-mesh namespace
scenarios.then: a row for chaos-mesh namespace exists with purpose 'Chaos Mesh fault injection platform'

tasks.title: Create Chaos Mesh HelmRepository source (REQ-001, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml with a HelmRepository CR pointing to https://charts.chaos-mesh.org. Use apiVersion source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1, namespace flux-system, interval 1h. Include SPDX Apache-2.0 header. Follow the exact format of sources/openbao.yaml (no comments beyond header, --- separator after header).

tasks.title: Create Chaos Mesh HelmRelease (REQ-002, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml with a HelmRelease CR. Use apiVersion helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2, namespace chaos-mesh, interval 30m. Set dependsOn [{name: cert-manager, namespace: cert-manager}]. Chart spec: chart chaos-mesh, version >=2.6.0 <4.0.0, sourceRef HelmRepository chaos-mesh in flux-system. Set install.crds CreateReplace, install.createNamespace true, upgrade.crds CreateReplace, upgrade.remediation.retries 3. Include SPDX header and descriptive comment matching the openbao.yaml pattern. Feature reference: CC-0046.

tasks.title: Add chaos-mesh namespace to namespaces.yaml (REQ-003)
tasks.description: Append a new YAML document to deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml with apiVersion v1, kind Namespace, metadata.name chaos-mesh. Follow the exact format of existing namespace declarations (--- separator, 3-line resource). Place it after the last existing namespace (openbao-system).

tasks.title: Add chaos-mesh resources to kustomization.yaml (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Edit deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml to add sources/chaos-mesh.yaml to the HelmRepository sources section (after sources/prometheus-community.yaml) and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml to the HelmRelease operators section (after releases/keystone-operator.yaml). Verify kustomize build deploy/flux-system/ succeeds.

tasks.title: Add kind overlay patch for Chaos Mesh (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Add a new patch entry to deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml targeting the chaos-mesh HelmRelease (group helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io, version v2, kind HelmRelease, name chaos-mesh, namespace chaos-mesh). The patch should set values: chaosDaemon.runtime=containerd, chaosDaemon.socketPath=/run/containerd/containerd.sock, dashboard.create=false, and reduced resource requests for controllerManager and chaosDaemon. Follow the strategic merge patch format used by existing patches (openbao, memcached-operator, keystone-operator). Include a descriptive comment explaining why the patch exists. Verify kustomize build deploy/kind/base/ succeeds.

tasks.title: Add chaos-mesh to deploy-infra.sh wait list (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Edit hack/deploy-infra.sh to add chaos-mesh to the Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases call (line 543-544). Add it to the end of the existing release name list: prometheus-operator-crds openbao mariadb-operator-crds mariadb-operator external-secrets memcached-operator chaos-mesh. No other changes to the script are needed — the existing wait_for_helmreleases helper handles chaos-mesh automatically. Update the script's Step 4 Phase 3 log message to mention Chaos Mesh if desired.

tasks.title: Add Chaos Mesh assertions to infra-stack-health E2E test (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Edit tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml to add Chaos Mesh health assertions. In Step 1 (Operator Deployments), add an assert for Deployment chaos-controller-manager in namespace chaos-mesh with (availableReplicas > 0) == true. Add a new Step (after Step 1) to assert DaemonSet chaos-daemon in namespace chaos-mesh with (numberReady >= 1) == true. Update the file's header comment to include Chaos Mesh in the Covers list. Follow the existing JMESPath assertion pattern.

tasks.title: Update infrastructure-manifests.md reference documentation (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Edit docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md to add Chaos Mesh entries: (1) Update Directory Layout to include sources/chaos-mesh.yaml and releases/chaos-mesh.yaml, (2) Add chaos-mesh to the Namespaces table with purpose 'Chaos Mesh fault injection platform for chaos engineering tests', (3) Add chaos-mesh row to HelmRepository Sources table, (4) Add Chaos Mesh subsection to HelmRelease Operators with target namespace, chart, version constraint, source, dependencies, and Helm values table, (5) Add chaos-mesh to the dependency order diagram, (6) Add chaos-mesh to the HelmRelease-HelmRepository cross-reference table, (7) Update resource counts in the Kustomization section.

implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This feature adds Chaos Mesh as a new FluxCD-managed operator following the identical pattern used by cert-manager, openbao, external-secrets, and memcached-operator. No new patterns or abstractions are introduced.

```
deploy/flux-system/
├── namespaces.yaml          ← ADD chaos-mesh Namespace
├── kustomization.yaml       ← ADD sources/chaos-mesh.yaml + releases/chaos-mesh.yaml
├── sources/
│   └── chaos-mesh.yaml      ← NEW HelmRepository → https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
└── releases/
    └── chaos-mesh.yaml      ← NEW HelmRelease (chaos-mesh ns, depends cert-manager)

deploy/kind/base/
└── kustomization.yaml       ← ADD patch: containerd runtime, no dashboard, minimal resources

hack/deploy-infra.sh         ← ADD chaos-mesh to Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases

tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/
└── chainsaw-test.yaml       ← ADD Chaos Mesh controller + daemon assertions

docs/reference/
└── infrastructure-manifests.md ← UPDATE with Chaos Mesh entries
```

## Data Flow

1. `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base` → creates Namespace chaos-mesh, HelmRepository chaos-mesh, HelmRelease chaos-mesh (with kind overlay patches)
2. FluxCD source-controller polls https://charts.chaos-mesh.org every 1h → downloads chart index
3. FluxCD helm-controller waits for cert-manager Ready → installs chaos-mesh chart into chaos-mesh namespace
4. Chaos Mesh installs: controller-manager Deployment, chaos-daemon DaemonSet (one pod per node), CRDs (PodChaos, NetworkChaos, etc.)
5. hack/deploy-infra.sh waits for chaos-mesh HelmRelease Ready=True before proceeding
6. infra-stack-health E2E test asserts controller-manager and chaos-daemon are running

## Dependency Order

```
cert-manager  (base — no dependencies)
├── openbao              dependsOn: cert-manager
├── mariadb-operator     dependsOn: cert-manager, mariadb-operator-crds
├── external-secrets     dependsOn: cert-manager
├── memcached-operator   dependsOn: cert-manager, prometheus-operator-crds
└── chaos-mesh           dependsOn: cert-manager  ← NEW
```

## Key Patterns to Follow

- **fluxcd-helmrelease-common-installupgrade-settings**: interval 30m, install/upgrade CRDs CreateReplace, remediation retries 3, createNamespace true
- **two-phase-kustomization-for-crd-dependent-resources**: Chaos Mesh is Phase 1 (base) since it installs its own CRDs via the Helm chart
- **spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files**: 3-line YAML header on all files
- **infrastructure-deployment-script-with-8-step-orchestration**: chaos-mesh added to existing Phase 3 wait list

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **Chaos Mesh Deployment name**: The controller Deployment is named `chaos-controller-manager` (not `chaos-mesh`). Verify via chart inspection or CI run before hardcoding in E2E assertions.
2. **DaemonSet name**: The chaos-daemon DaemonSet is named `chaos-daemon`. It must run on all kind nodes for fault injection to work.
3. **Kind containerd socket path**: Default is `/run/containerd/containerd.sock` for kind. If this changes in future kind versions, the overlay patch must be updated.
4. **Chart version constraint**: Use `>=2.6.0 <4.0.0` to allow 2.x and 3.x releases while blocking hypothetical breaking 4.x changes.
5. **No install.remediation on all existing releases**: Not all existing HelmReleases have install.remediation.retries. cert-manager, openbao, and external-secrets omit it. memcached-operator includes it. Follow the majority pattern (include it for robustness).

## Chaos Mesh Chart Registry

The official Chaos Mesh Helm chart repository is at https://charts.chaos-mesh.org. This is an HTTPS-type registry (not OCI), matching the pattern of cert-manager, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, and openbao sources.
